channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: The Afghanistan-Pakistan Border Is Still Unstable
item.description: There is a brief lull in violence, but the root causes of the conflict haven’t been addressed.

item.title: India’s Cockroach Movement Gains Momentum
item.description: A satirical political party has tapped into the discontent among unemployed youth.

item.title: Iran Launches Deadly Attack on Kuwait’s Main Airport
item.description: Experts warn that continued strikes could jeopardize peace talks.

item.title: What to Know About Ethiopia’s Election
item.description: Abiy’s all-but-certain victory threatens to destabilize the African nation.

item.title: Why Is Trump Targeting Brazilian Gangs?
item.description: The U.S. designation of two Brazilian crime groups as terrorist organizations scrambles already tense bilateral relations.

item.title: American capitalism has taken an apocalyptic turn
item.description: Millenarian thinking permeates business and markets

item.title: Meet the jailscraper
item.description: With no room for prisons, New York City is building up

item.title: Rocket goes boom; so do moon plans
item.description: The phenomenal explosion could blow a hole in Amazon’s plans and NASA’s too

item.title: The New Weapon Behind Ukraine’s Battlefield Success
item.description: Mid-range drones that can hit targets almost 100 miles behind Russian lines have changed the game.

item.title: Why France is uneasy about German rearmament
item.description: Germany could become the benchmark military power in Europe

item.title: Uzbekistan’s New Migration Destination? America.
item.description: From irregular border crossings to bilateral training agreements, Uzbekistan is working to carve out a formal pathway to U.S. employment.

item.title: Congress Prepares for a Consequential Iran Vote
item.description: Tehran may feel empowered to wait out U.S. demands for concessions in face of war-weary American public.

item.title: BYD is losing its spark
item.description: The Chinese electric-vehicle giant is struggling to stay ahead in an industry defined by software

item.title: Even if America and Iran find an accord, don’t expect it to last
item.description: The Donald Trump Show could be back on air later this year

item.title: The U.S. and Taiwanese Militaries Can’t Really Fight Together
item.description: A joint campaign to defend the island would struggle to operate effectively.

item.title: Trump’s New AI Order Raises the Stakes in China-US Tech Competition
item.description: Advanced AI models are no longer treated simply as commercial products; they are increasingly regarded as strategic assets linked to national power.

item.title: Democracy vs. Data Centers
item.description: As tech companies race to build data centers, communities and lawmakers across the United States are pushing back. Industry leaders frame local opposition as a threat to American competitiveness, but China’s experience shows that unchecked expansion carries serious risks of its own.

item.title: America Enabled the Gulf’s African Adventurism
item.description: Why Washington let its Middle Eastern partners wreak havoc in Africa.

item.title: The Right Incentives for Climate Action
item.description: As the closure of the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt global oil and gas flows, countries are increasingly focusing on short-term energy priorities to the detriment of long-term decarbonization goals. In such a context, the difficult task of designing climate policies that realign incentives takes on new importance.

item.title: Neutralizing Chokepoints: Lessons From the Hormuz Strait, Malacca, and Baltic Sea
item.description: Insights from Pekka Virkki.

item.title: EBRD Dials Down Kyrgyz GDP Forecast Over Sanctions Worries
item.description: While still growing, Kyrgyzstan’s GDP may slow due to increasing weight of sanctions.

item.title: Can a Takaichi Government Stabilize China-Japan Relations?
item.description: What Abe's post-2014 rapprochement with Beijing can teach Tokyo today.

item.title: The US and India Have Become Regional Rivals
item.description: While the US views India as a counterweight to China in Asia, it balks at the idea of Indian regional dominance, preferring a pluralistic order. The Trump administration has gone further by pursuing close ties with Pakistan, apparently hoping that the US and India can remain global partners.

item.title: Exit Polls Suggest Ruling Democratic Party’s Victory in Local Elections
item.description: Exit polls suggest the DP will win 11 of 16 regional posts, with Seoul and Busan producing the night’s most closely watched results.

item.title: Digital Sovereignty Is in the Fine Print
item.description: Despite efforts to build their own digital industries and infrastructure, most countries remain dependent on American tech firms for the provision of core government services. But government officials can tip the balance of bargaining power by attaching certain standardized provisions to public procurement and services contracts.

item.title: Bangladesh’s Twin Challenges: Economic Revival and Great Power Balancing
item.description: Bangladesh’s security lies not in becoming part of someone else’s strategic rivalry, but in becoming economically strong, diplomatically flexible, and internally legitimate.

item.title: If it’s true, go
item.description: Our leader from 1998

item.title: Middle East Cease-Fires Matter Even When They’re Broken
item.description: De-escalation, rather than lasting peace, is still important.

item.title: Trump Wants to Privatize His Presidential Records
item.description: By enacting the Presidential Records Act in response to Richard Nixon’s malfeasance, the US Congress created a process to limit abuse of power and ensure accountability. Like Nixon, Donald Trump seems determined to avoid accountability, which has led his Justice Department to argue—poorly—that the PRA is unconstitutional.

item.title: Why Asia’s Not Buying What Hegseth’s Selling
item.description: A Shangri-La charm offensive collided with regional values.

item.title: The Inertia of Russia’s War
item.description: Why Putin can’t end the conflict.

item.title: Iran’s New Grand Strategy
item.description: How a remade Islamic Republic will reshape the Middle East.

item.title: America has six years to fix its disappearing Social Security trust fund
item.description: It is already beginning to run dry

item.title: America’s Social Security trust fund is disappearing
item.description: Legislators have just six years to fix things

item.title: The ASEAN-China AI Center: Innovation Boost or Agentic Disinformation Risk for Southeast Asia?
item.description: Deeper economic integration with Chinese AI advances brings real opportunity – and heightened risks of influence campaigns.

item.title: The International Organization for Mediation Is 1 Year Old. How Is It Faring?
item.description: The Hong Kong-based IOMed is testing a new path for international dispute settlement, but the road ahead is challenging.

item.title: In Inner Mongolia, China’s Assimilation Campaign Moves Online
item.description: A January 2026 report suggests Beijing is now targeting the digital communities that were Mongolian speakers’ last refuge – in a region that never posed a separatist threat.

item.title: The Trump-Xi Summit Produced Stability, But It Won’t Last Forever
item.description: The arc of geopolitics will bend back toward competition.
